title: carelia houstoniana
intro: Carelia houstoniana, also known as Texas sage or Hill Country penstemon, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States, specifically in the dry regions of Texas. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Carelia houstoniana include reducing fever, relieving anxiety and stress, and promoting healthy digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy among natural health practitioners. Carelia houstoniana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional herbal preparations to enhance its medicinal effects.
